summary: Postmodern American author, one of the 'four best living American writers' according to Harold Bloom (alongside Pynchon, Roth, and McCarthy). Known for novels exploring consumerism, media, technology, conspiracy, and Cold War America.

works.name: White Noise
works.description: DeLillo's breakthrough novel, set at the College-on-the-Hill. Jack Gladney, who invented the academic field of Hitler Studies but doesn't know German, narrates. His wife Babette secretly takes the experimental drug Dylar (obtained through an affair with Willie Mink / Mr. Gray) to combat her fear of death. The novel's second part centers on the 'Airborne Toxic Event' involving the chemical Nyodene D. Jack's friend Murray Jay Siskind gives a simultaneous lecture on Elvis's mother while Jack lectures on Hitler's mother. Murray takes Jack to see the 'most photographed barn in America.' The company SIMUVAC uses real disasters to prepare for simulations. Jack eventually shoots Willie Mink, and the novel ends with Wilder Gladney crossing a highway on a tricycle. Jack's son Heinrich debates whether it's raining.

clues.clue: Jack Gladney invented Hitler Studies but doesn't know German; set at the College-on-the-Hill
examples: Hitler-studies professor Jack Gladney set at The-College-on-the-Hill
Jack Gladney tries to hide the fact that he does not know German despite being a professor of 'Hitler studies'

works.name: Underworld
works.description: DeLillo's massive novel, whose prologue 'Pafko at the Wall' describes the 1951 'Shot Heard Round the World' home run. Bobby Thompson's baseball is caught by the Black boy Cotter Martin, stolen by his father Manx, traced by Marvin Lundy, and eventually bought by the waste management executive Nick Shay. J. Edgar Hoover attends the game and is fascinated by Pieter Bruegel's The Triumph of Death. Klara Sax has an affair with a teenager who becomes Nick Shay and attends a private screening of the Zapruder film. Nick's unfaithful wife is Marian. The graffiti artist Moonman 157 appears. The title story of The Angel Esmeralda collection is largely drawn from this novel, featuring the nuns Edgar and Grace. The novel describes the Northeast power outage of 1965.

clues.clue: Opens with the 'Shot Heard Round the World'; prologue was 'Pafko at the Wall'
examples: opens with the 'shot heard 'round the world' home run
its prologue was adopted from DeLillo's short story 'Pafko at the Wall'

works.name: Libra
works.description: DeLillo's historical novel depicting the life of Lee Harvey Oswald leading up to the JFK assassination. The archivist Nicholas Branch reconstructs the central event. DeLillo invents the characters Win Everett and T.J. Mackey. The novel compares the Warren Commission report to Joyce's Ulysses and includes lyrical passages about the U-2 plane and Oswald's time in 'the brig' at Atsugi, Japan.

clues.clue: Depicts the life of Lee Harvey Oswald / JFK assassination

examples: compares the Warren Commission report to Joyce's Ulysses
Don DeLillo described this document as 'the megaton novel that James Joyce would have written if he'd moved to Iowa City and lived to be a hundred'

works.name: Mao II
works.description: A novel about the reclusive author Bill Gray, who attempts to rescue a Swiss poet held by terrorists in Beirut. Bill's assistant Scott and Scott's lover Karen appear. Karen participates in a Unification Church mass wedding at Yankee Stadium at the novel's opening. The novel ends with the line 'The future belongs to crowds.' Bill learns he has a lacerated liver from veterinarians in a restaurant.

clues.clue: Reclusive author Bill Gray tries to rescue a Swiss poet held by terrorists in Beirut
examples: the reclusive author Bill Gray attempts to rescue a Swiss poet held by terrorists in Beirut

examples: Karen participating in a Unification Church mass wedding at Yankee Stadium

works.name: Falling Man
works.description: DeLillo's 9/11 novel about Keith Neudecker surviving the World Trade Center attacks. Keith has an affair with Florence Givens despite being married to estranged wife Lianne. Their son fears the imaginary 'Bill Lawton.' The title refers to a performance artist who re-enacts scenes from 9/11.

clues.clue: Keith Neudecker survives 9/11; title performance artist re-enacts scenes; title references a photograph
examples: Keith Neudecker surviving the attacks in Falling Man
the title performance artist re-enacting a scene from 9/11

clues.clue: Keith enters a sexual relationship with Florence Givens despite being married to the estranged Lianne; their son fears the imaginary 'Bill Lawton'
examples: a survivor of this event becomes a professional poker player after entering a sexual relationship with Florence Givens despite being married to the estranged Lianne

works.name: Cosmopolis
works.description: The billionaire Eric Packer spends an entire day crossing chaotic New York City in his limousine to get a haircut. He has sex with and is stunned by a female bodyguard. He kills his bodyguard Torval while losing his fortune. The novel was criticized by Michiko Kakutani as 'a long day's journey into tedium.'

clues.clue: Eric Packer crosses New York in a limo for a haircut; kills bodyguard Torval
examples: a trip across a chaotic New York in search of a haircut, made by the billionaire Eric Packer in his limousine
Packer also kills another of these type of people named Torval

works.name: The Angel Esmeralda (stories)
works.description: DeLillo's 2011 short story collection. The title story features the nuns Edgar and Grace who confirm a miracle. 'Human Moments in World War III' depicts two people orbiting Earth collecting intelligence, communicating with Colorado Command. 'Midnight in Dostoevsky' follows college students following a hooded man. 'Baader-Meinhof' features a woman visiting an art museum for three days. 'Pafko at the Wall' became the prologue to Underworld.

clues.clue: Title story features nuns Edgar and Grace; 'Human Moments in World War III'; 'Baader-Meinhof'; 'Midnight in Dostoevsky'
examples: the nuns Edgar and Grace, who confirm the miracle of the title dead girl
Two people orbit above the Earth to collect wartime intelligence

clues.clue: 'Pafko at the Wall' became prologue to Underworld
examples: whose story 'Pafko at the Wall' became the prologue to his novel Underworld

works.name: Point Omega
works.description: A 2010 novel in which the filmmaker Jim Finley tries to make a documentary about Richard Elster, one of the architects of the Iraq War. The novel features an experimental artwork: Hitchcock's Psycho slowed to last twenty-four hours.

clues.clue: Filmmaker Jim Finley; Richard Elster; Psycho slowed to 24 hours
examples: the filmmaker Jim Finley tries to make a documentary about Richard Elster, one of the architects of the Iraq War
Hitchcock's Psycho is slowed down to last twenty-four hours

works.name: The Names
works.description: 1982 novel set in Athens; risk analyst John Axton and archaeologist Owen Brademas investigate a cult that kills victims whose initials match the town names. Axton's son is named Tap. Published alongside Libra and White Noise by Library of America in 2023.

clues.clue: Risk analyst John Axton and archaeologist Owen Brademas investigate a cult that kills victims based on initials matching town names; set in Athens amid Iran Hostage Crisis anxieties
examples: a cult that kills victims based on their initials and the towns that they are in is investigated by the language-obsessed archaeologist Owen Brademas and the risk analyst John Axton, who moves from the U.S. to Athens
In 2023, the Library of America republished this anthropology-themed novel alongside the politically-themed Libra and the sociologically-themed White Noise

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: DeLillo is consistently identified as a postmodern American author, though he rejected the label 'postmodern novelist.' Harold Bloom listed him alongside Pynchon, Roth, and McCarthy as one of the four best living American writers. Other works include Running Dog (1978, reporter Moll Robbins searches for a pornographic film of Adolf Hitler) and Cosmopolis (adapted by David Cronenberg in 2012; Eric Packer worries about his asymmetric prostate).

examples: Harold Blooms counts this man alongside Pynchon, Roth and McCarthy as being one of the four best living American writers

clues.clue: Cosmopolis adapted by David Cronenberg into a 2012 film; Eric Packer worries about his asymmetric prostate
examples: David Cronenberg wrote, produced, and directed this 2012 film based on a Don DeLillo novel. In this film, currency speculator Eric Packer sits in his limo worrying about his asymmetric prostate

comprehensive_summary:
Don DeLillo (b. 1936) is one of the most prominent postmodern American novelists, though he rejected the label himself. Harold Bloom placed him alongside Pynchon, Roth, and McCarthy as one of the four best living American writers.

White Noise (1985) is DeLillo's most frequently clued work. Jack Gladney, who invented the academic field of Hitler Studies at the College-on-the-Hill but cannot speak German, narrates. (His wife Babette takes the experimental drug Dylar — obtained through an affair with Willie Mink (Mr. Gray) — to combat her fear of death. Dylar side effects: users confuse words with things (saying 'speeding bullet' causes ducking); Mr. Gray curls into fetal position when Jack says 'Falling plane.'.) The novel's second section centers on the 'Airborne Toxic Event' caused by a derailed train releasing Nyodene D. Jack's friend Murray Jay Siskind delivers a simultaneous lecture on Elvis's mother while Jack lectures on Hitler's mother and wants to create a full Elvis course; they visit 'the most photographed barn in America' where 'once you've seen the signs about the barn it becomes impossible to see the barn.' SIMUVAC uses real disasters to rehearse for simulations (Baudrillard influence). Jack eventually shoots Willie Mink and takes him to a hospital run by nuns. The novel ends with Wilder Gladney crossing a highway on a tricycle. Title refers to visual TV static. The supermarket is described as timeless, full of 'Waves and Radiation.' Jack hosts the first Hitler Studies Conference. A character plans to break the world record for longest time in a cage of venomous snakes.

Underworld (1997) is DeLillo's massive Cold War novel. Its prologue, originally published as 'Pafko at the Wall' (named for Dodgers outfielder Andy Pafko backpedaling at the wall), describes the 1951 'Shot Heard Round the World' game. Jackie Gleason eats too many hot dogs and vomits on Frank Sinatra's shoes at the game; J. Edgar Hoover is fascinated by a Life magazine reprint of Bruegel's Triumph of Death. Bobby Thompson's baseball is caught by Black boy Cotter Martin, stolen by his father Manx, traced by Marvin Lundy, and eventually bought by waste management executive Nick Shay ('waste is the secret history'). Klara Sax, the artist, had an affair with teenage Nick Shay; Nick's wife Marian is unfaithful. The Texas Highway Killer shooting is recorded on video by a child; anchorwoman Sue Ann Corcoran receives an on-air call from the killer. Nick visits an art installation in the desert of 230 decommissioned Air Force planes. The graffiti artist Moonman 157 appears. The novel describes the Northeast power outage of 1965.

Libra (1988) fictionalizes Lee Harvey Oswald's life; archivist Nicholas Branch reconstructs the JFK assassination using invented characters Win Everett and T.J. Mackey. DeLillo described the Warren Commission report as 'the megaton novel that James Joyce would have written if he'd moved to Iowa City and lived to be a hundred.'

Mao II follows the reclusive author Bill Gray (assisted by Scott and Scott's lover Karen) who tries to rescue a Swiss poet from Beirut terrorists; it opens with a Unification Church mass wedding at Yankee Stadium and ends 'The future belongs to crowds.' The Names (1982) follows risk analyst John Axton in Athens investigating a cult that kills victims whose initials match town names; archaeologist Owen Brademas is his colleague; Axton's son Tap appears; Library of America republished it with Libra and White Noise in 2023. Falling Man (2007) follows Keith Neudecker surviving 9/11; he has a sexual relationship with Florence Givens while married to estranged wife Lianne; their son fears the imaginary 'Bill Lawton.' Cosmopolis follows billionaire Eric Packer (who worries about his asymmetric prostate) crossing New York in a limo for a haircut; David Cronenberg adapted it in 2012. Point Omega depicts filmmaker Jim Finley documenting Iraq War architect Richard Elster; features Psycho slowed to 24 hours. Running Dog (1978) features reporter Moll Robbins searching for a pornographic film of Adolf Hitler. The Angel Esmeralda collection includes 'Pafko at the Wall' (later Underworld's prologue), stories about nuns Edgar and Grace, orbital spies, and art museum obsession.
